data.title: Lanvin Group Taps Former 3M Exec Han for CFO
data.description:
By Nicholas G. Miller

Lanvin Group named Jiyang Han, a former executive at 3M, as the new chief financial officer of the luxury fashion company, effective Nov. 1.

Han was an executive at Yuyuan and previously worked at 3M Company and Ivory Capital. Lanvin said Han has nearly two decades of experience in corporate finance, investment banking and global business management.

Han will succeed David Chan, whose previously announced departure will take effect Oct. 27.

Write to Nicholas G. Miller at nicholas.miller@wsj.com.

data.title: Coya Therapeutics Shares Fall After Stock Offering Disclosure
data.description:
By Freddy Sebastian

Shares of Coya Therapeutics were lower after disclosing a public offering of its common stock.

Its shares fell 12.8% to $6.09 in premarket trading Friday.

The company said it expects to grant underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 15% of the shares offered in the proposed offering at the public offering price per share, excluding underwriting discounts and commissions.

The clinical-state biotechnology company plans to use proceeds from the proposed public offering for working capital and other general corporate purposes, including funding its clinical development plan.

Write to Freddy Sebastian at freddy.sebastian@wsj.com

data.title: Atlantis Glory Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Atlantis Glory Inc., a company specializing in elevator technology research, development, sales, maintenance, and installation, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report provides a detailed overview of the company's financial performance and operational status, highlighting the challenges and future plans as it navigates a period of no revenue generation and ongoing expenses.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenue: $0. The company reported no revenue for the three and nine months ended September 30, 2025, and 2024.

Net Loss: $7,675 for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $7,350 for the same period in 2024. The net loss for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, was $23,854, compared to $23,835 for the same period in 2024.

Earnings per share, Basic and diluted: $(0.00) for both the three and nine months ended September 30, 2025, and 2024, indicating no change in earnings per share over the periods.

Business Highlights

Organizational History and Overview: Atlantis Glory Inc., formerly known as Shengshi Elevator International Holding Group Inc., focuses on elevator technology research, development, sales, maintenance, and installation. The company has undergone several name changes and restructuring events, including a significant share exchange agreement in 2019 and a name change in 2022 to align with new business directions.

Current Business Operations: As of the report date, Atlantis Glory Inc. has no ongoing operations generating revenue. The company is primarily incurring expenses related to maintaining its corporate structure and compliance with SEC reporting requirements.

Future Business Plans: Management intends to explore and identify business opportunities, including potential acquisitions of operating entities through reverse mergers, asset purchases, or similar transactions. The company is considering business combinations with entities needing additional funds for development or expansion.

Operational Challenges: The company faces uncertainties and risks, including the impact of the coronavirus pandemic on the U.S. and global economies, which may hinder its ability to identify and implement a viable business plan.

Capital and Financing Needs: Atlantis Glory Inc. anticipates needing additional capital to fund operations and potential business combinations. The company may seek equity or debt financing, although there is no assurance of obtaining funds on acceptable terms.

Management's Outlook: The management acknowledges the complexity and risks associated with selecting a business combination, especially given the current economic conditions and competition for business opportunities. The company plans to fund its working capital requirements through existing funds and future securities issuances.

SEC Filing: Atlantis Glory Inc. [ AGLY ] - 10-Q - Oct. 24, 2025

data.title: India's NACL slips after Q2 profit drops on one-time expense
data.description:
** Agrochemical firm NACL Industries closes 3.86% lower at 208.25 rupees after Q2 results

** Co's net profit after tax slumps to 25.5 million rupees from 149 million rupees a year ago due to a one time expense of 174.5 million rupees in Q2

** NACL says one time expense recorded due to decision to discontinue certain products under development and other investments, after a strategic review by the board

** YTD, NACL shares have more than tripled

data.title: UK's Energean inks 15-year transmission deal for Israeli gas pipeline
data.description:
British gas producer Energean said on Friday it had signed a 15-year transmission agreement for Israel's planned Nitzana pipeline to Egypt, as part of its broader $4 billion worth of Israeli gas offtake contracts.

The deal with state-owned pipeline operator Israel Natural Gas Lines covers the transmission of up to 1 billion cubic metres of natural gas per year, Energean said, with options for extension and early termination.

The Nitzana pipeline will run from Ramat Hovav in southern Israel to the Egyptian border near Nitzana and is expected to be operational within three years, pending transmission agreements from all parties involved, Energean said.

The pipeline would ease an energy crisis in Egypt, which has spent billions of dollars on importing liquefied natural gas and is part of a concerted effort to boost Israeli gas exports to the Arab world's most populous nation.

It is expected to transport around 600 million cubic feet of natural gas per day, bringing Israel's total export capacity to Egypt to more than 2.2 billion cubic feet per day.

Energean Israel will fund 16.4%, or around $100 million, of the pipeline and compression station construction costs, the company said.

The company also signed a non-binding term sheet with an East Mediterranean client for future gas offtake.

data.title: Palm Oil Falls on Cautious Sentiment — Market Talk
data.description: Palm oil closed lower. Market sentiment likely remained cautious amid weak crude oil prices, high vegetable oil inventories in major consuming markets, trade tensions and a buildup of global soybean stocks, AmInvestment Bank writes in a note. The Bursa Malaysia Derivatives contract for January delivery fell 51 ringgit to 4,420 ringgit a ton. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

data.title: TSLA: Tesla Stock Climbs Back After Q3 Miss Sparks Sell-Off
data.description:
Oct 23 - Tesla stock slid about 4% on Thursday morning trading, but stock somehow able to recover gaianing more than 2% on closing bell after the company reported third-quarter earnings that fell short of expectations, despite a revenue beat.

However, Investors appeared to refocus on the company's long-term growth prospects and upcoming product pipeline.

Revenue rose 12% year on year, marking Tesla's first increase in three quarters. Net income, however, fell 37% from a year earlier, reflecting lower vehicle prices and a roughly 50% jump in operating expenses, including spending on artificial intelligence and other research projects.

Investors reacted cautiously, sending shares down in after-hours trading. The report followed disappointing results from Netflix and Texas Instruments , whose stocks dropped about 10% and 5.6% respectively during Wednesday trading.

Tesla CEO Elon Musk highlighted the company's ongoing AI initiatives, including work on Optimus, but did not provide fourth-quarter guidance for electric vehicles.

Broader U.S. markets pulled back, with major indexes retreating before partially recovering. Oil prices climbed nearly 3% after the White House imposed sanctions on Russian crude companies, adding pressure to energy and automotive costs.

Analysts noted that Tesla's profit margin pressures may continue unless operating costs stabilize or vehicle pricing improves against rising competition from Chinese manufacturers.

data.title: Macau Inflation Hits 8-Month High
data.description:
The annual inflation rate in Macau rose to 0.47% in September 2025, the highest since January 2025, accelerating from 0.27% in the previous month.

On a monthly basis, consumer prices edged up 0.1% in September, following a 0.11% rise in August.

data.title: MidWestOne Financial soars on $864 million buyout deal with Nicolet Bankshares
data.description:
** Community lender MidWestOne Financial Group's shares jump 29.9% to $36.85 premarket

** Larger peer Nicolet Bankshares will buy MOFG in an $864 million all-stock deal, the banks announced late on Thursday

** MOFG shareholders will receive 0.3175 share of NIC for each share of MidWestOne they own, valuing the bank at $41.37 apiece

** Per-share offer represents 45.8% premium to MOFG's last close; deal values MidWestOne at a price-to-tangible book value (TBV) per share of 166%

** TBV is a financial metric used to measure a company's net worth

** "We believe this deal checks several strategic boxes and brings rare attractive financial implications of 37% EPS accretion with only 1% TBV dilution," Piper Sandler says

** As of last close, MOFG shares down 2.6% YTD

data.title: India stocks, rupee, swaps, call at 3:30 p.m. IST
data.description:
STOCKS:

The benchmark BSE Sensex was down 0.41% to 84,211.88, while the broader NSE Nifty 50 index declined 0.37% to 25,795.15, as investors booked profits and as caution over India-U.S. trade weighed.

RUPEE:

The Indian rupee was little changed versus the U.S. dollar to 87.845, as trade tensions were back on the radar after comments from an Indian minister about ongoing negotiations turned the tide in favour of dollar bulls.

GOVERNMENT BONDS:

The benchmark 10-year bond (IN063335G=CC) was quoted at 98.51 rupees, with the yield largely unchanged at 6.5467%, as investors stayed away from purchases citing uncertain interest rate outlook amid thin volumes.

OVERNIGHT INDEX SWAPS:

The one-year overnight index swap rate (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) was down 1 bp at 5.455%, while the five-year swap rate (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) dropped 2 basis point to 5.65%.

CALL MONEY:

India's overnight call money rate (INROND=) was at 5.1% and the overnight TREPS rate (INTREPTOT=TCCL) was at 5.75%.

data.title: Rupee rally fizzles as cautious trade comments temper optimism
data.description:
By Dharamraj Dhutia

The Indian rupee surrendered all of its intraday gains to end little changed on Friday as optimism over trade talks faded after an Indian minister signalled caution over ongoing negotiations.

The rupee had earlier risen to 87.6350 per dollar, its highest level since late August, breaking past the key resistance level of 87.70 on likely inflows and the lingering impact of the central bank's persistent dollar sales near the 88 mark.

The currency ended at 87.8450, little changed from 87.8400 on Thursday.

However, things took a turn after Trade Minister Piyush Goyal said India will not rush into signing any trade agreements and will reject conditions from countries that restrict its trading choices.

"The first half of the session belonged to rupee bulls on the back of foreign inflows, but the second half saw dollar bulls claw back," a dealer at state-run bank said.

India is negotiating a long-pending free trade agreement with the European Union, while trade talks with the U.S., which has imposed 50% tariffs on domestic goods, are also underway.

"The rupee opened higher as we head for a deal with U.S. with expectations of reducing tariffs. But with Piyush Goyal commenting that India will not accept conditions or rush through trade deals, the rupee lost most of its gains," said Anil Bhansali, head of treasury at Finrex Treasury Advisors.

Traders also said that a pickup in equity inflows have supported the rupee in last few days. Foreign investors are net buyers of Indian shares so far in October.

Meanwhile, Asian currencies were mixed on Friday, with traders awaiting the release of September U.S. inflation data later in the day. The report was originally scheduled for October 15 and has been delayed due to a U.S. government shutdown.

Markets have fully priced in a 25 basis-point rate cut by the Federal Reserve next week, with the data expected to shape expectations for another reduction in December.

data.title: Samsung Heavy Industries' New-Order Outlook Deemed Encouraging — Market Talk
data.description: Samsung Heavy Industries' new-order outlook appears encouraging, Nomura analysts Eon Hwang and Heesoo Min say in a note. The shipbuilder is likely to secure two contracts worth about $2 billion each in 4Q. With the company recently receiving a $1.4 billion containership order from Evergreen, the analysts expect it to meet Nomura's 2025 new-order forecast of $11.3 billion by year-end. Management also seemed confident about 2026 prospects, supported by the resumption of LNG carrier orders and the company's sole-vendor position in building floating LNG facilities, they add. Nomura maintains the stock's neutral rating and raises its target to KRW23,000 from KRW22,000, factoring in the potential benefits for Korean shipbuilders from potential tighter U.S. regulations on Chinese shipyards. Shares last closed at KRW24,800. (jason.chau@wsj.com)

data.title: Reagan 1987 address was defence of free but fair trade
data.description:
The 1987 radio address by the late former U.S. President Ronald Reagan at the centre of a new U.S.-Canada row was a defence of free but fair trade in which he explained his decision to put duties on Japanese goods in a trade dispute.

U.S. President Donald Trump said on Thursday that all trade talks with Canada were terminated following what he called a fraudulent advertisement by the Canadian province of Ontario in which Reagan was featured speaking negatively about tariffs.

Separately, the Ronald Reagan Presidential Foundation issued a statement saying the ad used "selective audio and video" and misrepresented the address.

Reagan's 5-minute statement contains a strong defence of the benefits of free trade while explaining his decision the week before to impose tariffs on Japan due to its "inability to enforce their trade agreement with us on electronic devices called semiconductors."

"We expect our trading partners to live up to their agreements. As I've often said: Our commitment to free trade is also a commitment to fair trade," he said.

He added that while trade restrictions of any kind were a step he was "loath to take", the U.S. had evidence in the "special case" of unfair trade practices by Japanese companies.

What follows is the lengthy warning of the dangers of trade protectionism used in the Ontario ad, in which Reagan cites how it contributed to the economic misery experienced in the 1930s Great Depression.

"High tariffs inevitably lead to retaliation by foreign countries and the triggering of fierce trade wars," he said of a spiral that leads to higher prices and less competition. "Then the worst happens: Markets shrink and collapse; businesses and industries shut down; and millions of people lose their jobs".

He concludes by looking forward to imminent talks with the Japanese premier on the matter and warns against any moves in Congress towards more protectionism that would tie his hands in trade dealings with foreign governments.

He added: "Remember, America's jobs and growth are at stake."

data.title: 13 Hotel Branding Strategies That May Improve Guest Loyalty and Market Recognition - Digital Silk Branding Agency Shares Insights
data.description:
Miami, Florida--(Newsfile Corp. - October 24, 2025) - Digital Silk, an award-winning agency focused on creating brand strategies, custom websites, and digital marketing campaigns, has released new insights on modern hotel branding, highlighting 13 strategies that may support stronger guest connections, recognition, and repeat stays.

13 Hotel Branding Strategies That May Improve Guest Loyalty and Market Recognition - Digital Silk Shares Insights

To view an enhanced version of this graphic, please visit:

https://images.newsfilecorp.com/files/10732/271429_17298bf414f64a1e_001full.jpg

Why Brand Consistency Matters for Hotels

According to TechSci Research, the U.S. hotels market is projected to reach approximately $395.8 billion by 2030, reflecting continued competition across both mid-range and luxury segments. Strong branding can help hotels clearly define their market position and build trust through consistent experiences.

"Our analysis shows that consistent branding across visuals, messaging, and guest experience can support long-term brand relevance and loyalty," said Courtney Bozigian, Head of Brand Development at Digital Silk.

Core Findings from the Report

Digital Silk's insights explore practical ways hospitality brands can strengthen recognition and differentiation:

  • Define a Clear Value Proposition: Establish a brand promise that aligns with a distinct guest segment, such as family-focused stays, wellness retreats, or business travel
  • Maintain Consistency Across Touchpoints: Uniform visuals, tone, and digital presence may improve recall and reinforce guest confidence
  • Avoid Common Brand Missteps: Over-promising, inconsistent digital experiences, and imitation of competitors can weaken brand identity

Three Steps Hotels Can Take Now

Hotels aiming to strengthen market recognition may consider:

  • Conducting a brand audit to measure current guest perception
  • Aligning brand visuals, tone, and service experience across all channels
  • Using digital platforms strategically to communicate their core identity

Key Takeaways

  • A cohesive brand identity may improve guest retention and trust
  • Visual, experiential, and digital factors all contribute to perceived value
  • Avoiding branding inconsistencies can protect long-term reputation

More insights are available at https://www.digitalsilk.com/digital-trends/hotel-branding-strategies/.

About Digital Silk

Digital Silk is an award-winning Branding Agency focused on growing brands online. With a team of seasoned experts, Digital Silk creates digital experiences through strategic branding, custom web design, and digital marketing services to help improve visibility and support engagement.

Media Contact

Jessica Erasmus

Marketing Director & PR Manager

Tel: (800) 206-9413

Email: jessica@digitalsilk.com

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/271429

data.title: Qualigen jumps on announcing $30 mln crypto treasury partnership with BitGo
data.description:
** Shares of drugmaker Qualigen Therapeutics rise 83% to $6.45 premarket

** Co late on Thursday said it partnered with digital asset infrastructure company BitGo to manage a $30 mln crypto treasury allocation

** BitGo to provide custody and desk services for investment and management of digital assets - QLGN

** Co said strategy involves investing in top 10 cryptocurrencies, excluding stablecoins

** QLGN, majority-owned by EV startup Faraday Future , says "partnership with BitGo allows us to further diversify our corporate treasury with digital assets, reflecting our commitment to both financial resilience and leadership in the evolving digital economy"

** In September, FFAI and QLGN partnered to launch a crypto and Web3 business

** Up to last close, stock down ~16% YTD
